title: Bangladesh National Cybersecurity Strategy 2021
description: Bangladesh's National Cybersecurity Strategy 2021 establishes the strategic framework for protecting Bangladesh's cyberspace, critical information infrastructure, and digital economy. The Strategy is implemented by the Digital Security Agency and sets out goals, objectives, and action plans across the key areas of governance, protection of critical infrastructure, incident response, capacity building, and international cooperation.

provisions.title: Chapter 1. Vision, Mission and Strategic Objectives
provisions.content: The Vision of Bangladesh's National Cybersecurity Strategy is: a secure, resilient and trusted cyberspace that enables Bangladesh's socioeconomic development and protects the rights of its citizens. The Mission is: to establish and maintain a comprehensive national cybersecurity ecosystem that effectively protects Bangladesh's critical information infrastructure, government digital services, and the digital economy from cyber threats, while fostering trust in digital services and enabling innovation. Strategic Objectives: (1) Strengthen the national cybersecurity governance framework; (2) Protect critical information infrastructure and government digital services; (3) Build national cybersecurity capacity and awareness; (4) Develop a vibrant cybersecurity industry; (5) Promote international cooperation in cybersecurity; (6) Foster a culture of cybersecurity in society. The Strategy covers the period 2021-2025 and shall be reviewed mid-term in 2023 to ensure it remains relevant to the evolving threat landscape.

provisions.title: Chapter 2. Cybersecurity Governance
provisions.content: Effective cybersecurity governance requires clear roles, responsibilities, and accountability across all sectors of government and society. The National Cybersecurity Governance Framework consists of: (a) National Cybersecurity Council: a high-level body chaired by the Prime Minister to provide strategic direction and oversight; (b) Digital Security Agency (DSA): the lead agency for cybersecurity policy, strategy, and operational coordination; (c) BGD e-GOV CIRT: the national computer incident response team for government networks and critical infrastructure; (d) Sector-specific cybersecurity coordinators in critical sectors; (e) Private sector and civil society advisory bodies. The governance framework shall ensure that cybersecurity is integrated into all aspects of government planning and operations. Ministerial cybersecurity coordinators shall be appointed in each ministry to serve as the focal point for cybersecurity within their ministry's portfolio.

provisions.title: Chapter 3. Critical Information Infrastructure Protection
provisions.content: Protecting Bangladesh's critical information infrastructure (CII) is a cornerstone of the National Cybersecurity Strategy. Critical sectors identified in Bangladesh include: electricity generation and distribution; oil and gas; water and sanitation; financial services and banking; telecommunications and internet; transportation; healthcare; and government services. A CII Protection Framework shall be developed prescribing minimum security standards for each critical sector. Sector-specific Computer Emergency Response Teams (CERTs) shall be established for the financial sector (FinCERT), the telecommunications sector, and other critical sectors. CII operators shall: implement the minimum security standards prescribed by DSA; report significant cyber incidents to BGD e-GOV CIRT within specified timeframes; maintain business continuity plans; and cooperate with DSA inspections and audits. A national CII Risk Assessment shall be conducted every two years.

provisions.title: Chapter 4. Incident Response and Recovery
provisions.content: Bangladesh shall maintain robust national cyber incident response capabilities to detect, respond to, and recover from cyber attacks. The BGD e-GOV CIRT shall be developed into a full-capability national CERT with: 24/7 incident monitoring and response; malware analysis and reverse engineering capabilities; digital forensics capabilities; a threat intelligence platform for sharing information on threats; and coordination mechanisms with sector-specific CERTs and international CERTs. A National Cyber Incident Response Plan (NCIRP) shall be developed and tested through regular exercises. The NCIRP shall define: incident classification criteria; escalation procedures for incidents of different severity levels; roles and responsibilities of all stakeholders in incident response; communication protocols with the public and media during significant incidents; and recovery procedures. Bangladesh shall participate in regional and international cyber exercises to test and improve its response capabilities.

provisions.title: Chapter 5. Cybersecurity Capacity Building
provisions.content: Building national cybersecurity capacity is essential for the long-term security of Bangladesh's cyberspace. Capacity building initiatives include: (a) Developing cybersecurity education programs at all levels, from school to postgraduate; (b) Establishing a National Cybersecurity Training Center offering professional certifications; (c) Training 10,000 cybersecurity professionals over the next five years; (d) Developing a government cybersecurity workforce program to recruit, train and retain cybersecurity talent in public service; (e) Establishing partnerships with international cybersecurity academies and training providers; (f) Supporting university research centers in cybersecurity; (g) Developing a national cybersecurity awareness campaign targeting citizens and businesses. Women's participation in cybersecurity shall be actively promoted through scholarships, mentoring programs, and targeted recruitment. Bangladesh shall develop a Cybersecurity Innovation Hub to connect researchers, startups, and industry partners.

provisions.title: Chapter 6. Cybersecurity Industry Development
provisions.content: Bangladesh shall develop a domestic cybersecurity industry capable of meeting the nation's security needs and contributing to the economy. Industry development initiatives include: (a) establishing a Cybersecurity Technology Park providing space and support services to cybersecurity companies; (b) creating procurement preferences for domestic cybersecurity products and services in government contracts; (c) providing venture capital and other financing support to cybersecurity startups; (d) promoting Bangladesh as a destination for cybersecurity research and development; (e) facilitating the import of cybersecurity tools and technologies needed for the domestic market. The DSA shall work with industry associations to develop a Bangladesh Cybersecurity Certification Mark for products and services meeting specified security standards. Bangladesh shall promote the export of cybersecurity services to regional markets. Collaboration between the cybersecurity industry and academic institutions shall be facilitated through joint research programs.

provisions.title: Chapter 7. International Cooperation
provisions.content: Bangladesh recognizes that cyber threats are transnational and that international cooperation is essential for effective cybersecurity. International cooperation objectives include: (a) establishing cyber diplomatic relationships with key partner countries; (b) concluding bilateral cybersecurity cooperation agreements with strategic partners; (c) participating actively in regional cybersecurity mechanisms, including those of SAARC and BIMSTEC; (d) engaging constructively in the United Nations processes for developing international norms for responsible state behavior in cyberspace; (e) contributing to international capacity building efforts for developing countries; (f) maintaining trusted operational relationships with international CERTs for incident response cooperation. BGD e-GOV CIRT shall become a member of FIRST (Forum of Incident Response and Security Teams). Bangladesh shall leverage the United Nations, Commonwealth, and other multilateral forums to advocate for international cybersecurity norms that protect the interests of developing countries.

provisions.title: Chapter 8. Cyber Awareness and Education
provisions.content: Creating a cybersecurity-aware culture is essential for Bangladesh's long-term cybersecurity. National cyber awareness initiatives include: (a) a 'Digital Safety Bangladesh' campaign targeting different segments of society with tailored messaging; (b) integration of cyber safety education into the national school curriculum from primary level; (c) training for teachers and university faculty on cybersecurity education; (d) providing free online cybersecurity awareness resources for the general public; (e) targeted awareness programs for at-risk groups including children, women, and elderly persons; (f) cybersecurity awareness programs for businesses, focusing on SMEs; (g) training for journalists on cybersecurity reporting. October shall be observed as Cybersecurity Awareness Month in Bangladesh. The DSA shall maintain a Cyber Helpline providing advice and assistance to citizens and businesses affected by cyber incidents. DSA shall publish an annual Bangladesh Cybersecurity Report documenting the state of cyber threats and the progress of cybersecurity implementation.

definitions.definition: Digital systems, networks and information resources in sectors vital to national security, public health, safety, or economic stability, whose disruption or destruction would have significant adverse consequences.

Bangladesh Government's Computer Incident Response Team, the national CERT operating under the Digital Security Agency, responsible for responding to cybersecurity incidents affecting government networks and critical infrastructure.

Financial sector Computer Emergency Response Team, a sector-specific CERT for the banking and financial services sector in Bangladesh, coordinating cybersecurity incident response and threat information sharing.
